title: Find the Source Defect behind a Forgiving Render
summary: Browsers recover from many source errors, so a visible page is not proof of conformance. A disciplined review combines an HTML validator, source inspection, the parsed DOM, the accessibility tree, and a retest after the smallest root-cause repair. Predict, inspect, explain, and apply the contract in a cumulative public-information build.
objectives: Explain html validation tools with source and browser evidence.
Apply html validation tools without discarding earlier document work.
Diagnose the named misconception in a changed public-service scenario.

steps.title: Predict the pre-publication conformance review failure · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim should guide the first pre-publication conformance review decision before the page is opened? Work context: pre-publication conformance review.
steps.why: Find the Source Defect behind a Forgiving Render requires this decision because the learner must distinguish a specification-defined relationship from a browser-recovered appearance. This is checkpoint 1 in the pre-publication conformance review.

options.text: Pre-publication conformance review case 1: repair every visible symptom with extra div elements without reading validator output.
Pre-publication conformance review case 1: rendering and conformance are different evidence; validate source and trace each message to a root cause.
Pre-publication conformance review case 1: developer Tools DOM is the saved source, so differences between them prove the validator is wrong.

content.text: Reconstruct the pre-publication conformance review sequence by dependency, not by how familiar each term looks.

options.text: pre-publication conformance review phase 2.1: reproduce the problem and preserve the failing source

pre-publication conformance review phase 2.4: apply the smallest repair and rerun the complete evidence set

steps.title: Build the pre-publication conformance review increment · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 8: Repair the cumulative source so its main region closes before the body and the complete document closes once. Work context: pre-publication conformance review.
steps.why: Find the Source Defect behind a Forgiving Render keeps earlier work active while adding one testable HTML increment. This is checkpoint 8 in the pre-publication conformance review.
